raw_text: 1. Judge me, O Lord; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the Lord; therefore I shall not slide. 2. Examine me, O Lord, and prove me; try my reins and my heart. 3. For thy lovingkindness is before mine eyes: and I have walked in thy truth. 4. I have not sat with vain persons, neither will I go in with dissemblers. 5. I have hated the congregation of evil doers; and will not sit with the wicked. 6. I will wash mine hands in innocency: so will I compass thine altar, O Lord: 7. That I may publish with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works. 8. Lord, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth. 9. Gather not my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloody men: 10. In whose hands is mischief, and their right hand is full of bribes. 11. But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity: redeem me, and be merciful unto me. 12. My foot standeth in an even place: in the congregations will I bless the Lord.
distilled_principles: Moral integrity requires deliberate separation from corrupting influences and associations
Authentic worship demands consistency between public devotion and private conduct
Those who maintain ethical standards can confidently invite divine scrutiny of their lives
ethics_reasoning: This psalm strongly emphasizes virtue ethics through its focus on integrity, truthfulness, and righteous character. The deontological element is significant in the psalmist's commitment to moral duties regardless of social pressure. Justice appears in the contrast between the righteous and those who take bribes, while commonsense morality underlies the rejection of evil associations.
